question_text: Why does a balloon expand when you heat the air inside it?

options.label: B
options.text: Heating makes the rubber thinner and weaker

options.label: C
options.text: Increased temperature causes particles to move faster, increasing pressure, which pushes the balloon outward

options.label: D
options.text: Hot air is lighter and floats up

correct_answer: Increased temperature causes particles to move faster, increasing pressure, which pushes the balloon outward
explanation: When you heat the air inside a balloon, the particles gain energy and move faster. Faster-moving particles collide with the balloon walls more frequently and forcefully, creating greater pressure. This increased pressure pushes outward on the balloon, causing it to expand.
